## [decision: cipro interpretability audit → bounded falsifier coordination (parallel Claude↔Codex)] 2026-05-22
**Salience:** HIGH
**Session:** freeform
**Modules:** wiki/cipro_bounded_falsifier_coordination_plan_2026-05-22.md (new), wiki/cipro_bounded_falsifier_subset_2026-05-22.json (new), scripts/leakage_check_dup_accession.py (new), reports/cipro_interp_audit_analysis_2026-05-22.md (Claude's analysis memo of Codex's audit), wiki/current_cipro_interpretability_audit_2026-05-21.{json,md} (Codex's audit on Precision 7780).
**Corrections:** /brainstorm caught probability-Δ saturation as alternative to lineage confound. Original memo headlined "the 45% ELX-family near-clonal block confounds gene-level ISM attribution" — refined to "batch/clade-associated failure mode; cause not identifiable from artifact alone" after adversarial review pointed out median Δ ≈ 0.001 on ELX is consistent with BOTH lineage-confound (signal smeared across embedding) AND classifier saturation (P(R) ≈ 1.0 → all single-gene drops are tiny by construction). Refined to add diagnostic-export requirements gated on the falsifier.
**Reversals:** Originally proposed "fix duplicate accession (cosmetic)" — reclassified as potential LOSO same-genome leakage (`GCA_025200635.1` = `562.109860` AND `562.111036` by byte-identical audit results; LOSO puts each strain_id in the other's training set by construction). Snippet `scripts/leakage_check_dup_accession.py` ships as <5s gate that blocks falsifier interpretation if `loso_leakage_present=True`.
**Discoveries:** (a) Parallel-session collaboration across machines (Codex CLI on Precision 7780 with the model + cache; Claude on GTX 860M laptop with analysis + plan generation) needs an explicit coordination contract — roles, file-transfer cadence, naming convention, escalation triggers — checked into the repo, not held in either session's transient context. (b) The 12-strain falsifier subset selection logic separates 3 failure modes (control ERS / failure-case ELX-family / all-negative-Δ) so a SINGLE method change (positive-only Δ ranking) is testable against all 3 in parallel. (c) v0 ship-or-document-scope-limit fork is symmetric — PASS path runs Mash-cluster + method refinement; FAIL path ships v0 WITH a documented `attribution_scope_confidence` field. Either outcome ships per the north star ("AI DNA decoder tool, not papers").
**Lesson:** When a saturation regime is mathematically possible for an attribution method, probability-scale deltas alone under-discriminate; report logit deltas + max-abs-Δ across all genes as a side-channel BEFORE drawing confound conclusions. Cross-engine adversarial review (here: /brainstorm) is cheap insurance against motivated-reasoning headlines that survive a single-engine read. Corollary on parallel-session collaboration: write the coordination contract as a checked-in artifact with explicit roles + file cadence + escalation triggers + verdict matrix — the 10-min upfront cost pays back the first time the other session would have emitted a divergent artifact name or pass criterion.

## [plan_file: Stage1_Refactor_And_Test_Hardening_Plan.md] Executed 2026-05-14
**Mode:** sequential (user-chosen over parallel due to concurrent N=40 NT populate consuming D: drive I/O) | **Result:** All 3 steps completed
**PRs:** N/A (direct commits to main: 3190d90, 0542347, 197f999)
**Salience:** HIGH
**Modules:** scripts/stage1_n40_cipro.py, dna_decode/eval/{cv.py,loso_kmer.py (new),verdict.py}, dna_decode/models/classical_baselines.py, tests/{test_stage1_runner.py,test_calibration_discipline.py,test_verdict_semantics.py,test_alignment.py}, plans/Stage1_Refactor_And_Test_Hardening_Plan.md
**Notable:** All 3 steps clean. Test count 386 -> 399 passed / 1 deselected, 0 regressions. Background N=40 NT cache populate (started ~17:30 UTC, ~5-7h ETA) and smoke gate regression check ran throughout but were not waited on; D: drive I/O contention was the reason sequential mode was chosen over parallel. The load-bearing event for this session happened BEFORE /execute-plan ran: a post-/save-plan /brainstorm pass caught 4 critical + 3 medium issues in the saved plan; the plan file was edited in-place to absorb all 7 corrections before worktree agents touched code, so execution itself was uneventful.
**Corrections:** none from user during execution. The pre-execute /brainstorm corrected the plan itself in 7 places (see Reversals).
**Reversals:** Pre-execute plan corrections (all caught by /brainstorm before any code edit): (1) stale API — `run_kmer_xgboost_loso(cohort, refseq_root)` signature would have silently changed Stage 1's strain subset/order vs the smoke gate; (2) verdict semantics drift — proposed CI-lower-bound rule was promoting NOISY -> FAIL inside the verdict bucket, conflicting with the stated non-goal of mixing confidence into the verdict label; (3) `contig_separator` import would fail — no module-level export existed, needed explicit `CONTIG_SEPARATOR` constant added; (4) `_train_baseline_logreg(calibrate=False)` needed an explicit non-calibrated branch, not just a kwarg passthrough; (5) calibration discipline tests missed the k-mer-XGB gate-bearing call site; (6) `CVResult` lacked a `strain_ids` property the runner refactor assumed; (7) fusion variant alignment-mismatch handling should suppress the diagnostic note (not raise) to keep the gate evaluable. All seven were absorbed into the plan file via in-place edit before /execute-plan started.
**Discoveries:** (a) The smoke gate (committed 2026-05-14 as 8e0fe18) had been written with `calibrate=False` already plumbed at NT-XGBoost; the Stage 1 plan had only specified it for the NT path and missed the k-mer-XGB symmetric call site — /brainstorm catch prevented an asymmetric calibration regime between two gate-bearing paths. (b) The `silent mean-fallback on ClassifierTrainingError` pattern was load-bearing in the legacy runner — the LOSO discipline contract required removing it on the NT path (NaN-emit instead) while keeping the k-mer/fusion paths re-raising; without explicit test coverage this asymmetry would have been invisible to future refactors. (c) Verdict-vs-action separation: the original plan conflated "what was measured" (verdict: PASS/NOISY/FAIL by point-gap) with "what to do next" (stage2_action: PROCEED/INVESTIGATE/STOP, allowed to weight CI/secondary signals) — the /brainstorm correction split them into two fields, making verdict a pure function of measurement and isolating decision-layer logic.
**Lesson:** When a result drives a downstream decision, keep the result label (what was measured) and the decision label (what to do) as SEPARATE fields. Conflating them invites motivated reasoning at decision-time — "the CI is wide so let's call it FAIL" silently redefines what the verdict means and makes the historical record incomparable. Verdict semantics should be a pure function of the measurement; the decision layer can incorporate confidence/CI/secondary signals without redefining the verdict. Corollary specific to /technical-plan workflow: when /brainstorm catches >=3 issues in a saved plan, edit the plan file in-place BEFORE /execute-plan rather than carrying corrections as a separate delta — worktree agents read only the plan file, and a delta document they don't load will let them re-create the exact bugs the brainstorm caught.

## [plan_file: Sidework_Sequence_Ship_Path_Plan.md] Executed 2026-05-14
**Mode:** sequential | **Result:** All 6 steps completed
**PRs:** N/A (direct commits to main: 66dfde2, 2ca1799, 9538ef8)
**Salience:** HIGH
**Modules:** dna_decode/models/{foundation,cache}.py, dna_decode/data/, tests/, pyproject.toml, scripts/, docs/, wiki/, plans/, research_outputs/, TODOS.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, LESSONS_LEARNED.md, FUTURE_FEATURES.md, ~/.claude/.../user_environment.md
**Notable:** Three execution-time surprises required mid-step pivots: (1) TODOS.md hunk discipline broke — three logically separate change classes lived in a single git hunk, so `git add -p` was insufficient; recovery used revert + edit-tool-based progressive restoration across A/C/E. (2) Step M's premise ("replace RTX 4090 in auto-memory") was wrong — user_environment.md never had RTX 4090; pivoted to adding a positive GPU-reality entry instead. (3) ARCHITECTURE.md per-line judgment confirmed 2 `4-bit` references at lines 79/118 are workflow descriptions (not current-feasibility claims) and were left as-is. Final test count 359 passed / 1 skipped / 0 failed / 0 warnings; `slow`-marker warning eliminated via pyproject.toml registration. N=40 cipro cohort (20R/20S, 5-strain margin) built cleanly; D12's margin choice paid off vs. D's original exact-25 plan.
**Corrections:** none (user-driven), but multiple plan premises required runtime correction (see Notable + Discoveries)
**Reversals:** TODOS.md staging strategy shifted from `git add -p` (planned) to revert + progressive edit-tool restoration (executed) once the single-hunk reality was confirmed. Step M pivoted from replacement to addition once auto-memory grep showed no RTX 4090 entry to replace.
**Discoveries:** (a) TODOS.md had no pre-existing line 17 modification — plan's D3 already caught half of this (edit-then-stage), but the deeper finding was that A/C/E hunks were not separable via `git add -p`; (b) user_environment.md had no RTX 4090 claim — the auto-memory edit was additive only; (c) Cross-engine convergence (Codex CLI + ChatGPT) on the B-B + deterministic CRC32 hashing decisions was load-bearing as a confidence multiplier — neither engine alone would have surfaced both the singleton-MLST degeneracy AND the PYTHONHASHSEED reproducibility trap.
**Lesson:** Before scheduling a "replace X with Y in file F" step, verify F currently contains X. Plans framed as cleanup/correction frequently encode a premise that the stale content exists; grep the target before committing the step to a wave, or the step pivots from "replace" to "add" mid-execution and the wave-graph time estimate misfires. Additionally: when staging touches a multi-class file (TODOS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, README.md), assume hunks are NOT separable by `git add -p` until `git diff <file>` proves otherwise — single-hunk reality forces revert + progressive edit restoration, which is slower than the plan's `add -p` line implies.

## [retrospective: smoke-gate calibration overcorrection — verdict reversal FAIL → PASS] 2026-05-14
**Salience:** HIGH
**Session:** freeform
**Modules:** scripts/smoke_gate_12strain_cipro.py (new, 380 LOC), dna_decode/models/classifiers.py (calibrate path — read-only investigation), wiki/smoke_gate_12strain_cipro_2026-05-14.md (result packet), plans/Phase2_Decision_Gate_Plan.md (4→3 variant rescope), TODOS.md (3 new [OPEN] entries), LESSONS_LEARNED.md (3 new 2026-05-14 entries), CLAUDE.md (smoke-command + calibrate note)
**Corrections:** none direct. Indirect via /brainstorm: my initial "plot PCA/UMAP first" recommendation was reordered to "fix gene-presence bug first" — Codex flagged that bug-fix is BOTH high-info AND blocking, so it must precede the cheaper-but-non-blocking diagnostic. "Highest info per minute" was the wrong objective when one action removes a hard dependency.
**Reversals:** Smoke-gate verdict reversed from FAIL → PASS after investigation. Initial run with default `calibrate=True` returned NT-XGBoost AUROC=0.000 with only two output values (0.463 for R, 0.537 for S — perfectly symmetric and rank-inverted across 12 LOSO folds). Looked like "NT broken on real data"; would have demoted the NT track. Real cause: `CalibratedClassifierCV` with isotonic regression at N=11 training collapsed to symmetric two-value output. Re-run with `calibrate=False` recovered AUROC=0.750 (NT leads k-mer by +5.6 pp). Smoke verdict flipped from FAIL → PASS. Plan also reversed from 4 variants → 3 (AMRFinder dropped) when execution-time check showed the cohort's persisted `plasmid_resistance_genes` / `chromosome_resistance_genes` fields are empty for the 12-strain mini cohort and no per-strain AMRFinderPlus CLI driver exists.
**Discoveries:** (a) `CalibratedClassifierCV` + isotonic regression at N=11 training collapses to two-value symmetric output that rank-inverts predictions — produces AUROC≈0.000, looks like model failure, is actually calibrator failure. (b) Gene-presence + XGBoost ALSO returned AUROC=0.000 even with `calibrate=False` already set — different mechanism (probably XGBoost overfitting on N=11 train × thousands of sparse binary gene-ID features), still pending root-cause. (c) The locked Phase2_Decision_Gate_Plan assumed `pipeline.py train` could handle classical-baseline variants; it can't (foundation-model-only path). Had to build `scripts/smoke_gate_12strain_cipro.py` runner (380 LOC) before smoke could run. (d) Cohort's persisted resistance-gene fields are EMPTY across all 12 mini-cohort strains, blocking the AMRFinder variant via cohort-data substitution.
**Lesson:** AUROC≈0.000 with perfectly symmetric two-value scores is a signature of a CALIBRATION / LABEL-COLUMN / ROW-ORDER / CLASS-MAPPING bug — NOT "model broken on real data." Genuine model failure produces noisy random-ish scores, not perfectly-symmetric two-value collapse. Investigate the calibration path + score-column selection before drawing any model-quality conclusion. Corollary: when ranking next-actions, weight by "blocks vs informs" — blocking bug-fixes precede diagnostic plots/visualizations even when more expensive; "highest info per minute" is the wrong objective when one action removes a hard dependency.

---

## [retrospective: Phase 1 evidence collection CLOSED — cross-drug architectural finding + audit-infrastructure pattern] 2026-05-17
**Salience:** HIGH
**Session:** freeform
**Modules:** wiki/ep1_ep2_cross_drug_architectural_finding_2026-05-17.md (synthesis), wiki/cipro_ep1_closeout_2026-05-17.md, wiki/EP2_cef_tet_verdict_2026-05-17.md, wiki/cipro_decision_bundle_pre_conditions_2026-05-17.md, wiki/cipro_mechanism_audit_2026-05-17.md, wiki/cipro_mic_audit_2026-05-17.md, wiki/cipro_mechanism_phenotype_audit_2026-05-17.md, scripts/cipro_mic_audit.py (new), scripts/cipro_mechanism_audit.py (new), scripts/cipro_mechanism_phenotype_merge.py (new), scripts/cipro_curated_baseline.py (new), scripts/smoke_gate_12strain_cipro.py (patched), data/processed/gate_b_mini_{cef,tet}_cohort.parquet (new), plans/Cipro_Decision_Bundle_{Plan,Technical_Plan}.md (new), plans/Cef_Mechanism_Audit_Plan.md (new, revised v1→v4), plans/EP1_EP2_Cross_Drug_Synthesis_Plan.md (new), project_state/dna-decode-2026-05-11.md (Action Log rows 40-54 + Bellman frame refreshed twice).
**Corrections:** none from user as text overrides; all corrections surfaced via sequential /brainstorm + /review cycles and were absorbed in-place per the 2026-05-14 HIGH-salience LESSON (edit plan files in-place BEFORE /execute-plan, not as delta docs).
**Reversals:** MAJOR — the cef mechanism audit plan went through 4 revisions in one session (v1 rejected 11 steps → v2 reduced 3 → v3 re-inflated 8 after round-2 detail accumulation → v4 reduced 3 again after round-3 framing critique). Plus originally-proposed PC2 60% threshold for "label-noise concentration" was caught as BELOW the uniform-error null baseline (K/N = 26/38 ≈ 68.4%) — would have biased the EP1 closeout toward "labels are the problem" regardless of evidence; replaced with Fisher-exact label-stratified statistical-context schema. Plus H13 NT-LR anti-predictive AUROC 0.100 root cause was feature-scaling (LR overwhelmed by unscaled 512-dim NT embedding magnitudes); fix was `Pipeline(StandardScaler, LR)` wrap; recovered to 0.673 (57-pp recovery).
**Discoveries:** (a) Cross-drug evidence chain (cipro + cef + tet at smoke fidelity) made the mechanism-class-bounded architectural partition visible at 3 drugs in a way single-drug deep-dive could not. NT-frozen-pooling PASSES on concentrated-signal mechanisms (cipro QRDR + cef plasmid β-lactamases) AND FAILS on distributed mobile-element mechanisms (tet efflux + ribosomal protection); pattern appears largely independent of drug identity at smoke fidelity. (b) The 4-tier audit infrastructure built for cipro EP1 (mechanism × MIC × opacity merge with structurally-enforced SUSPEND gate) generalizes to any drug — cef + tet mini cohorts built from existing N=38 cipro cohort strain pool, reusing the populated NT cache without re-populate; only cosmetic output-string patches needed in the smoke runner. (c) Smoke-runner had a silent-variant-drop bug (`try/except Exception` swallowing FileNotFoundError on missing GFF3) that propagated through 2 publishable smoke reports — fix deferred to maintenance mode. (d) Anti-predictive AUROC at N=12 with calibrate=False is data-shape divergence (genuine architectural mismatch), distinct from the 2026-05-14 LESSON on calibration overcorrection at N=11 (symmetric two-value scores). Both failure modes look anti-predictive but have different score-distribution signatures.
**Lesson:** When a project's model-evaluation pipeline returns inconclusive results, the load-bearing next experiment is often an ADVERSARIAL AUDIT INFRASTRUCTURE on cohort labels + tool outputs, NOT a deeper model experiment. EP1 cipro's Stage 1 FAIL looked like "model didn't work" until the mechanism × MIC × opacity merge audit revealed the cohort itself was unusable (clean_count = 7 of 40; opacity_count = 0; signal_quality = 0.17). The structurally-enforced `SUSPEND_CONDITION_4` gate prevented a Databricks burst on uninterpretable labels AND revealed that the architectural finding could be grounded in cross-drug evidence (cipro + cef + tet at smoke fidelity) at zero additional model-training cost. Corollary on cross-drug chains: when the load-bearing question is architectural, prefer adding the next drug at smoke fidelity (cosmetic output-string patches; shared NT cache; mini cohort filtered from existing strain pool) over chasing N=150 cohort expansion on the first drug. The marginal cost is hours, not days; the discriminating power is high.

---

## [retrospective: brainstorm-cycle decision-avoidance pattern + plan-language drift catch] 2026-05-17
**Salience:** HIGH
**Session:** freeform
**Modules:** plans/Cef_Mechanism_Audit_Plan.md (revised v1→v2→v3→v4 within one session), plans/EP1_EP2_Cross_Drug_Synthesis_Plan.md (sanity-checked, M1+M2 absorbed in-place), wiki/ep1_ep2_cross_drug_architectural_finding_2026-05-17.md (final synthesis with §3 falsification trigger + conservative cef framing).
**Corrections:** none direct; instead, the /brainstorm + /review skills repeatedly caught the user's own pattern (reaching for more planning skills after the synthesis was declared done) and explicitly flagged it 5 times across rounds: round 3 framing critique ("stop building plans, start writing"); round 4 sanity check ("after this pass, more /brainstorm IS decision avoidance"); /technical-plan invocation flagged BEFORE building ("synthesis is sequential prose work; /technical-plan formalism doesn't add value"); /save-plan invocation flagged BEFORE saving ("/save-plan creates new file; brainstorm explicitly said don't save more plans"); /review meta-flag ("if you find yourself wanting to apply all 9 edits + then re-review, that's the decision-avoidance pattern again — recognize it + ship").
**Reversals:** Plan-language drift on the cef plan v1→v4 cycle. v3 (8 steps) opened with "Smaller alternative to the rejected 11-step plan" — that phrase did two jobs: preserved the v1 /review's REDUCE authority while authorizing round-2 expansion that produced PC pre-conditions + Mash-clade baseline + Wilson CI + 4-cell decision matrix. /brainstorm round 3 caught the language-doing-two-jobs pattern; v4 reduced back to 3 steps. Lesson learned at the framing layer: when a plan describes itself relative to a prior review's verdict ("smaller alternative", "more focused", etc.), audit the actual size against the verdict's spirit, not the prose framing.
**Discoveries:** (a) The /brainstorm pattern reliably surfaces different concerns by round depth — round 1 tactical (verdict bands, gate inversion, wrong NT head), round 2 strategic (PC pre-conditions, evidence object, Fisher-exact threshold), round 3 framing ("smaller alternative" language preserving prior-review authority), round 4 sanity (apply 2 final polish edits). The 2026-05-15 HIGH-salience LESSON ("don't stop at round 1; round 4's adversarial target is your own framing assumptions") was empirically validated 3 more times this session. (b) /brainstorm itself is a tool whose invocation can become decision-avoidance once the work is done. The stopping-rule discipline must be NAMED + ENFORCED EXPLICITLY in the artifact (synthesis plan D3's narrowly-scoped reopen trigger) because human pattern-compulsion will keep reaching for the next planning skill otherwise. (c) Counter-discipline tool: AskUserQuestion to flag the pattern BEFORE running the invoked skill. Used 4× this session; each time the user confirmed the smaller move (in-place edit + ship) rather than the full planning skill.
**Lesson:** When a /brainstorm or /review surfaces "no critical issues" + flags continued review-cycling as the next risk, the discipline-honoring move is to ship the artifact, not to invoke the next planning skill. Specifically: if a plan/artifact is declared closeout-ready AND the next user instinct is /save-plan / /technical-plan / /review on that same artifact, flag it as the decision-avoidance pattern BEFORE running the skill. Offer the smaller move (apply N obvious in-place edits + commit) explicitly. Corollary on plan-language drift: when a revised plan opens with "smaller alternative to [rejected prior]", measure the step count + scope against the prior verdict's spirit, not the prose framing. "Smaller alternative" doing two jobs (preserve review authority + authorize similar expansion) is a real catchable failure mode — count steps + read both plans side-by-side.

---

## [retrospective: phase-ladder → Evidence Packets framing reset + Stage 2 toolchain install + verify_complete cache integrity gate] 2026-05-15
**Salience:** HIGH
**Session:** freeform
**Modules:** project_state/dna-decode-2026-05-11.md (37 Action Log rows + EP1-EP5 Mid-term + Candidate-actions refresh + 12 Decisions Made + 9 Pending Decisions + H1-H4/H11/H15/H16 status normalizations + H17 added), dna_decode/models/cache.py (verify_complete + CompletenessReport), dna_decode/eval/phylogeny.py (O(N^2) → 2-mash-call refactor; use_docker kwarg), tools/docker_runner.py (new module + 9 tests), scripts/probe_nt_cache.py (new CLI), tests/test_models_cache.py (8 verify_complete tests; total 42), tests/test_eval_phylogeny.py (19 tests; was 13), tests/test_docker_runner.py (9 tests), wiki/stage2_install_artifact_2026-05-15.md, plans/Stage2_Docker_Tools_Install_Plan.md, plans/EP2_Cef_Tet_Smoke_Design_Plan.md, LESSONS_LEARNED.md (+3 entries), prior-session diagnostics tracked.
**Corrections:** none from user as text overrides; sustained adversarial /brainstorm cycle (4 rounds against my own synthesis) corrected the project-management framing in 7 places: (1) Refined goal staleness — chr21 era goal never `--supersede-goal`'d after E. coli pivot 2026-05-11; (2) "Phase 1 shipped" semantic overload — infrastructure-shipped, not science-shipped, per wiki/phase1_ship_report.md; (3) my proposed Phase 1.5 / 2 / 2.5 / 3 / 4 / 5 / 6 ladder was a phase-label addition on top of the existing collision, not a fix; (4) "platform" framing in long-term goal was too strong for solo/part-time constraints; (5) cipro-first sequencing for cef + tet was scientifically under-tested — should be parallel-fire smoke, not sequential after cipro Stage 2 PASS; (6) H17-H21 proposal mixed hypotheses + feasibility gates + backlog items (only H17 + H18 concrete enough); (7) Mid-term Phase-table inconsistent with Pending-Decisions-row-8 framing reset after the initial 7-mutation batch — needed table replacement, not annotation.
**Reversals:** MAJOR — mid-session approach reframing. Phase-ladder abstraction (Phase 1.5 / 2 / 2.5 / 3 / 4 / 5 / 6) replaced with Evidence Packets matrix/spiral hybrid (EP1 cipro Stage 2 / EP2 cef+tet smoke parallel / EP3 attribution audit / EP4 clade-shift / EP5 cef+tet Stage 2 — plus parked horizon tracks). Stage 2 toolchain install pivoted multiple times: Bakta v1.12.0 → v1.11.4 (AMRFinder dep-check regression), Git Bash → MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 (silent mount-bind failure), direct `--entrypoint /opt/conda/bin/bakta_db` → `--entrypoint /bin/bash -c "bakta_db ..."` (conda env activation skipped without shell init). Cipro-R smoke strain pin shifted from "any ST131 R" to specific accession `1328433.3 / GCA_000522345.1` after Codex flagged phenotype-R-without-textbook-mutations risk.
**Discoveries:** (a) `EmbeddingCache.populate(skip_existing=True)` skips at gene-dataset path level (cache.py:296), NOT strain level — half-flushed strain at crash time becomes valid-looking mean embedding via Stage 1's `cache.list_genes` + mean-pool. f9ed79f flush patch bounds loss to 1 strain but doesn't eliminate; consumer-side `verify_complete` + `probe_nt_cache.py` is the defense. (b) Smoke runner internal logic is already drug-parameterized (`drug_lower` from `--drug` arg at scripts/smoke_gate_12strain_cipro.py:75,128,165), only output paths cipro-hardcoded — EP2 lift is smaller than initial framing suggested. (c) AMRFinderPlus POINT* on ST131 cipro-R substrate (`GCA_000522345.1`) detected textbook gyrA-S83L/D87N + parC-E84V on the FIRST RUN — empirical confirmation that the comparator pipeline detects the canonical QRDR signature without further tuning. (d) Bakta v1.11.4 `bakta_db download` AMRFinder dep-check fails when invoked via `--entrypoint /opt/conda/bin/bakta_db` (skips conda env activation) but PASSES when wrapped in `--entrypoint /bin/bash -c "bakta_db ..."` (conda activates via login shell init). (e) Git Bash silently breaks Docker `-v <host>:/<container>` mounts via MSYS path conversion — `MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1` is required for direct `docker run` invocations; Python `subprocess.run` is unaffected (no shell to munge). (f) `executed_plans/` archival convention was inconsistent — Phase 1 plans used in-file `Status:` headers; only `Stage1_Refactor_And_Test_Hardening_Plan.md` was moved to the dir. (g) The Phase-N labeling system itself was the problem, not the content within it — load-bearing finding that emerged only at the 4th /brainstorm round when adversarial pressure shifted from "is the recommendation right?" to "is the framing assumption right?"
**Lesson:** When a project's labeling system (Phase 1 / 2 / 3, Wave A / B / C, Stage I / II / III) means different things in different artifacts within the same repo, the labels themselves become misleading — annotating them ("Phase 1 was infrastructure-only") is weaker than replacing them with semantically-distinct names ("Evidence Packets"). Corollary specific to long sessions with sequential /brainstorm cycles: each round drills from tactical → strategic → framing — round 4's adversarial target should be your own framing assumptions, not your synthesis content. Don't stop at round 1 even when round 1 produced an accepted recommendation; the deepest layer reveals whether the language you're using is itself doing two jobs.

---

## [retrospective: AMD Polaris mining-rig path tested + rejected] 2026-05-18
**Salience:** HIGH
**Session:** freeform
**Modules:** analyze_rig.bat, analyze_rig.ps1 (new diagnostic), Stress_Load_decoder*.py (Databricks notebook variants in user's Downloads), zed-claude-installer/ (existing, referenced for .tab Gmail workaround)
**Corrections:** none — direction shift driven by hardware reality, not user pushback.
**Reversals:** MAJOR. User offered a 12-GPU mining rig as a compute substrate during the Databricks-vs-Colab-vs-rig deliberation for the deferred N=147 cipro NT cache populate. Initial plan: build an analyzer .bat to detect GPU model + classify ROCm/CUDA viability; if NVIDIA-modern (RTX 20+) or AMD RDNA2/3 → use the rig + skip Databricks. Reality: user's photos (AORUS RX 580 8GB box + Sapphire NITRO card + open-frame rig) confirmed AMD Polaris (RX 570 + RX 580). ROCm officially dropped Polaris (gfx803) support after v4.5 in 2021; modern PyTorch (2.x) requires ROCm 5.x+. Rig path abandoned with no setup work spent. Pivoted back to Databricks burst.
**Discoveries:** (a) `analyze_rig.bat` v1 had cmd-batch fragility (nested if/goto + `del %GPU_TMP%` with unbound variable triggered a near-wildcard-delete prompt on the project root) — rewrote as thin `.bat → .ps1` launcher pattern; PowerShell is much more robust for hardware enumeration via `Get-CimInstance Win32_VideoController`. (b) Gmail blocks zips containing `.bat` / `.ps1` / `.exe` attachments — `.tab` rename workaround documented in the existing `zed-claude-installer/README.txt`; reused for the rig-setup zip + the Databricks-bundle zip. (c) Mining-era 8 GiB AMD GPUs are statistically RX 580 8GB (the ETH-era card), which is the exact card class ROCm blocks. The 5-minute visual-verification step (read the GPU box label) saved a multi-hour ROCm install attempt.
**Lesson:** Before committing to ANY compute-substrate setup work (ROCm install, CUDA install, dual-boot Linux, cluster config), verify the GPU model's architecture tier against the framework's support matrix. AMD: Polaris (RX 4xx/5xx) = dropped after ROCm 4.5; Vega + RDNA1 = marginal; RDNA2 = unofficial-but-workable; RDNA3 = officially supported. NVIDIA: pre-Pascal (CC<6.0) = blocked from modern PyTorch; Pascal (GTX 10xx, CC 6.1) = works but no Tensor Cores; Turing+ (CC≥7.5) = full support including bitsandbytes. Visual confirmation of the actual GPU model (box label, sticker, `nvidia-smi`, `rocminfo`) is the cheapest gate.

---

## [retrospective: Databricks DBFS FUSE mkdir EIO bug + workaround] 2026-05-18
**Salience:** HIGH
**Session:** freeform
**Modules:** Stress_Load_decoder*.py (Databricks notebook), data/processed/stage2_n150_cipro_cohort.parquet (bundle), dna_decode/ + scripts/ + config/ (bundle subset)
**Corrections:** none.
**Reversals:** Cell 2 of the Databricks notebook (Section 2 — "Resolve bundle path") failed on `Path("/dbfs/Decorder_AI_project").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` with `OSError [Errno 5] Input/output error`. Error trace: `pathlib.py:1313 os.mkdir(self, mode)`. The `/dbfs/` FUSE mount is read-mostly for native Python file I/O on modern Databricks runtimes (user's instance: ML 14.3 LTS GPU); `mkdir` fails even though `cohort.parquet` reads at `/dbfs/...` paths succeed. Rewrote storage strategy: use `/local_disk0/dna_decode_work/` for working storage (fast SSD, ephemeral) + push final `.h5` to `dbfs:/FileStore/Decorder_AI_project/embeddings/nt_n147_cipro.h5` via `dbutils.fs.cp` at end (REST API path; bypasses FUSE).
**Discoveries:** (a) The FUSE mkdir bug is path-creation-specific — file reads at `/dbfs/...` paths work, but `os.mkdir` / `pathlib.Path.mkdir` fail with EIO. (b) The bug is especially common in Unity-Catalog-enabled workspaces where `/dbfs/` is technically deprecated; the official direction is Unity Volumes (`/Volumes/<catalog>/<schema>/<volume>/`). (c) Working storage on `/local_disk0/` is bounded by cluster auto-terminate — set ≥240 min for the 2-3 hr A10G NT inference window; termination mid-run loses the working cache. The `dbutils.fs.cp` push at the end is the persistence step. (d) The user-named `Decorder_AI_project` outer Workspace folder is intentional (project obfuscation for work-laptop context); the inner Python package stays `dna_decode/` so imports use `from dna_decode...` regardless of the Workspace folder name.
**Lesson:** On Databricks, NEVER use `Path("/dbfs/...").mkdir(...)` or `os.makedirs("/dbfs/...")` from notebook code. Use the `dbutils.fs.*` REST API for all DBFS writes (or work on `/local_disk0/` and push at the end). For long-running ML workloads: working storage on cluster-local SSD with a single explicit `dbutils.fs.cp` push at the end is more reliable than streaming intermediate writes through the FUSE mount, AND auto-terminate must be set generously enough to survive the longest cell.

---

## [retrospective: BV-BRC strict-MIC 4-drug infeasibility + audit-framework-as-downstream-gate insight + v0 lock] 2026-05-18
**Salience:** HIGH
**Session:** freeform
**Modules:** scripts/bvbrc_strict_mic_4drug_census.py (new), wiki/bvbrc_strict_mic_4drug_census_2026-05-18.{md,json}, dna_decode/data/mic_tiers.py (new shared module + 76 tests), scripts/pipeline.py (predict v0 rewrite + 16+6 tests), wiki/decoder_v0_ux_and_success_criterion.md (LOCKED), README.md (+v0 quickstart section), plans/Phase2_Framing_Brainstorm_Plan.md + Technical_Plan (superseded by north-star clarification), memory/project_dna_decode_north_star.md (new)
**Corrections:** /review caught that strict-MIC at 4× safety margin was the WRONG QUESTION for an "AI DNA decoder tool" goal. Strict-MIC is paper-grade label-quality; the audit framework (mechanism × MIC × opacity merge with structurally-enforced SUSPEND gate, shipped Phase 1) already gates cohort quality downstream. Pre-filtering on strict-MIC duplicates the audit framework's job and discards usable training data. User clarified north star mid-session: "AI DNA decoder tool, no need for papers — failure-tolerant iteration." Rejected the structured Q1-Q3 framing brainstorm. Earlier 3-candidate research/audit-tier slate (`plans/Phase2_Framing_Brainstorm_Plan.md`) marked as superseded.
**Reversals:** Phase 2 entry plan reshaped. Original assumption: "build N=150 per-class strict-MIC cohorts for cipro+cef+tet+gent, run Stage 2 NT cache populates per drug, do per-drug architectural verdicts." Census revealed: NO drug clears N=150 per-class at strict-MIC OR relaxed-MIC. Cipro 17R/4S, cef 66R/2S, tet 1R/0S, gent 2R/132S. Structural bottleneck is `assembly_accession` (96% drop on cipro strict-MIC), NOT MIC strictness — relaxed-MIC adds 5 cipro + 2 gent DECISIVE strains, but all lack assemblies. The categorical N=147 cipro cohort (already built; Databricks burst populating its NT cache mid-session) is THE viable v0 substrate. Cef may be v0.1 substrate if cef-S labels can be backfilled from PATRIC/NARMS/EuSCAPE (deferred). Tet + gent dropped from Phase 2 candidate list. 4th-mechanism-class falsifier (gent) substrate also infeasible — needs a new candidate.
**Discoveries:** (a) **Architectural insight that emerged from the failure**: the audit framework RELAXES the cohort-construction bar. Pre-audit-framework, you needed clean labels because nothing downstream caught dirty ones. Post-audit-framework, permissive cohort + downstream audit gate is the right shape. This validates the audit framework as the project's most reusable artifact, not the classifiers. (b) Assembly_accession bottleneck pattern (CLAUDE.md previously documented qualitatively for cipro) confirmed empirically across all 4 drugs at population scale — clinical labs report MICs but don't always submit assemblies to NCBI. (c) Class-imbalance shape is drug-class-specific: β-lactam + quinolone are R-rich + S-sparse at strict-MIC; aminoglycoside is S-rich + R-sparse. Real biology + clinical-reporting pattern, not artifact. (d) `dna_decode/data/mic_tiers.py` shared module: per-drug breakpoints + AMRFinder Class filter + DRUG_LOCI_BY_MECHANISM (cef/tet/gent catalogs in addition to cipro) + classify_gene_symbol helper with tolerant-prefix-match. Migrated bvbrc_strict_mic_4drug_census.py to use it; left cipro_mechanism_audit.py as-is (cipro-specific by design + 26 existing tests). 76 new tests on the shared module. (e) v0 UX + success criteria LOCKED: CLI via `pipeline.py predict` (extends existing pattern), LOSO AUROC ≥ 0.70 (lower than Phase 1 ≥0.80 because strict-MIC isn't feasible), cipro v0 / cef v0.1, JSON + markdown sidecar output. 3 of 5 criteria fully green via 24 new tests (16 unit + 6 E2E with synthetic fixtures including a synthesized cache + classifier pickle + GFF3 + merge-gate JSON); 2 gated on the Databricks N=147 cache landing. (f) Total tests this session: 597 → 621 (+24). Zero regressions across all changes.
**Lesson:** When a project ships a downstream-noise-handling artifact (audit framework, anomaly detector, robust loss, data-quality gate), it RELAXES the upstream-data-purity bar. Don't pre-filter to academic-grade purity if the downstream artifact already handles noise — that's doing the same work twice AND discarding usable training data. Corollary: feasibility censuses should test the bar that matches the project's actual goal, not the strictest defensible bar. If the goal is "publication-grade benchmark," strict-MIC is right; if the goal is "tool that works on whatever exists + flags noise honestly," strict-MIC overshoots. Cross-corollary: when the user clarifies project intent mid-session (e.g., "tool not paper"), supersede prior framing artifacts EXPLICITLY (mark superseded + save the clarification to memory) rather than letting two competing frames coexist silently.

---
